name: aviation_security_water_allocation_stonehaven_lib_eq
premise: A Aviation Security response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving aviation security water allocation stonehaven lib eq. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is before regulators arrive. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as liberty vs equality under pressure. Every action is being logged and will be reconstructed in an after-action review. Constraints: Keep public statements strictly factual; label assumptions. Preserve evidence under audit and litigation-hold.

agents.name: Luc Ibrahim
agents.identity: I am Luc Ibrahim, accountable for a defensible call in Aviation Security when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays equality for liberty.

agents.name: Mei Kaur
agents.identity: I am Mei Kaur, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Aviation Security.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so equality isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of liberty.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect equality.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to equality to secure liberty under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is before regulators arrive. Evidence is partial. Luc Ibrahim must decide under liberty vs equality. What does Luc Ibrahim do?
